costs: Recurrent funding commitment for targeted school subsidies, against a backdrop of already low overall education spending (4.1% of government expenditure, rank 41/42) (F-live-education_finance-06)
Administrative cost of designing and monitoring a needs-based formula distinct from existing capitation funding
Teacher incentive costs amid a 16,000-teacher shortage that constrains redeployment capacity (F-live-implementation_planning-07)
content.equity_impact: Moderate and indirect: improves conditions and outcomes for disadvantaged students within the existing sorted system but does not change who gets selected into gimnázium tracks or reduce the segregation intensity between schools; functions as a mitigation layer rather than a structural fix
expected_benefits: Politically lower-cost than structural reform since it avoids confronting the free-choice constitutional guarantee, the directors' lobby, or Act CXC amendment (F-live-legal_and_governance-01, F-live-political_feasibility-07)
Directly targets documented resource and teacher-quality gaps in disadvantaged schools (F-live-education_finance-07, F-live-demography-09)

Consistent with the comparative finding that delayed tracking only improves equity when bundled with broader reform — this proposal instead substitutes broad non-structural bundling absent the age change
content.goal: Improve outcomes and narrow equity gaps for disadvantaged students without altering the 10-12 selection age or track structure, via targeted funding, curriculum clarity, and school-quality levelling

implementation_steps.action: Design and fund a targeted supplementary-funding formula for schools with high concentrations of disadvantaged students and/or high peer-outflow to selective tracks
implementation_steps.actor: Ministry of Education / Ministry of Finance

implementation_steps.action: Prioritize qualified-teacher placement and retention incentives in rural and disadvantaged-area feeder schools
Extend curriculum-clarity and workload-reduction reforms (already on the 2024 agenda) to all comprehensive lower-secondary schools with implementation monitoring

Establish an evaluation framework to track equity-gap indicators (PISA disadvantaged-student skill attainment, early school leaving) attributable to the compensatory package, addressing the documented lack of shared trusted baseline

mechanisms: Targeted (TEIP-style) supplementary per-pupil or per-school funding for schools serving disadvantaged populations, compensating for capitation losses when high performers depart (F-live-education_finance-05, F-live-education_finance-06)

Address teacher-quality gap in disadvantaged/feeder schools where unqualified-teacher proportion is 2-3x higher, via incentive funding and placement policy (F-live-demography-09, F-live-portuguese_reform-07)
Curriculum clarity and workload/memorization reduction reforms already proposed by the ministry, extended systematically to comprehensive-track schools (F-live-polish_reform-10)
Increase overall instructional time allocation, given Hungary ranks 32/34 OECD in lower-secondary instruction hours, as a lever independent of track structure (F-live-education_finance-06)
risks: Evidence record explicitly notes this class of reform has not been evaluated for impact on the equity gaps it targets, meaning outcomes are unproven (F-live-portuguese_reform-07)

Without altering the selection mechanism or track structure, high-performing peer and funding outflows continue unabated, so compensatory funding may only partially offset — not eliminate — the underlying structural loss (F-live-education_finance-05, F-live-school_network_planning-07)
Government has selectively invoked equity findings for legitimation while implementation has lagged in the past, raising credibility and follow-through risk (F-live-portuguese_reform-09)
Persistent within-school classroom sorting by SES may absorb some of the intended benefit, since sorting tends to relocate rather than disappear (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-10)
content.title: Non-structural compensatory equity package (Portugal-style, selection age unchanged)
